name: tiermux
description: Agentic AI coding assistant powered by the free tiers of 18+ LLM providers (and growing), with automatic failover and smart per-task model routing. Bring your own keys.

tiermux.agent.maxIterations.description: Maximum tool-call iterations per agent task before pausing to check in. Higher values let the agent complete multi-step tasks without stopping.

tiermux.agent.maxConcurrentRuns.description: Maximum number of chat sessions that run their agent at the same time. Extra sends queue and start as a slot frees, to respect free-tier rate limits.

tiermux.agent.requireWriteConfirmation.description: Show a diff and ask for confirmation before the agent writes/creates/deletes a file.

tiermux.agent.qualityGate.description: Escalate to the next model when an answer is weak (refusal, repetition, truncation, or too-short-for-task) instead of accepting it. Only fires when the fallback chain has another hop (never for a pinned model).

enumDescriptions: Ask for approval before every command (safest).
Auto-run a small set of safe inspection/test/build commands; ask for anything else.
tiermux.agent.commandApproval.description: How the agent's runCommand tool is gated before running shell commands.

tiermux.agent.commandTimeoutMs.description: Maximum time (ms) a single agent command may run before it is killed.

tiermux.agent.commandAllowlist.description: Extra command prefixes auto-runnable in 'allowlist' mode (e.g. "php artisan migrate"), in addition to the built-in safe defaults.

tiermux.agent.autoCompactThreshold.description: Automatically summarize (compact) the conversation when it exceeds this fraction of the model's context window. 0 disables auto-compact.

tiermux.memory.autoLearn.description: After edits, infer basic code-style signals (indent, quotes, semicolons) for free and record them in .tiermux/memory.md so future turns stay consistent. No model call — pure heuristics.

tiermux.graph.enabled.description: Enable the structural code graph (imports, exports, call edges, entrypoints). Provides cross-file context to the agent and enables getSymbolGraph, impactAnalysis, and onboarding tour tools. Off by default — opt in for refactor-heavy work (the regex-based graph can be imprecise on large codebases).

tiermux.tools.web.description: Give the agent webSearch and webFetch tools so it can look things up instead of guessing. On by default — adds the tool specs (and tokens) to every agent request.

tiermux.tools.searchEndpoint.description: Optional web-search endpoint URL for the webSearch tool. Blank = a best-effort free endpoint (fragile). Set to a reliable search API/SearchXNG URL for stable results; requires "tiermux.tools.web" to be on.

tiermux.tools.exaApiKey.markdownDescription: Exa AI API key for semantic web search (free tier: 1,000 searches/month). Get one at [exa.ai](https://exa.ai). Exa returns clean, structured content optimized for AI agents. Requires `tiermux.tools.web` to be on.

tiermux.tools.braveApiKey.markdownDescription: Brave Search API key (free tier: 2,000 queries/month). Get one at [brave.com/search/api](https://brave.com/search/api/). Provides reliable web search with no rate-limit issues. Requires `tiermux.tools.web` to be on.

enumDescriptions: Auto = use the first provider that has a key configured (Exa → Brave → custom endpoint → free DuckDuckGo). Recommended.
Free DuckDuckGo via duck-duck-scrape (no key needed, but often rate-limited).
tiermux.tools.searchProviderPriority.description: Which web search provider to use. 'auto' tries Exa → Brave → custom endpoint → free DuckDuckGo in order, picking the first with a key configured.

tiermux.context.includeOpenEditors.description: Automatically include the active file (a slice around the cursor) and open tab names as context each turn.

tiermux.context.ambientSliceRadius.description: Lines of context on each side of the cursor to include with the active file. Lower = fewer tokens per turn (helps free-tier quotas).

tiermux.context.ambientMaxChars.description: Hard cap (chars) on the active-file slice injected per turn. Lower = fewer tokens per turn.

tiermux.context.ambientMaxTabs.description: Maximum number of open tab names to include in ambient context.

tiermux.utilityModel.description: Model used for short utility tasks (chat titles, commit messages). 'auto' prefers a strong keyless model so it works without an API key; or pick a specific model in Settings → Others.

tiermux.plan.saveToFile.description: Save actionable plans proposed in Plan mode as markdown checklist files you can read and edit. The plan and its live task checklist are also shown in chat.

tiermux.plan.folder.description: Workspace-relative folder where plan files are written (one markdown file per plan).

tiermux.embeddings.rerank.description: Rerank semantic-search candidates for higher precision (uses Cohere /rerank when a Cohere key is set, otherwise a lexical blend).

tiermux.requestTimeoutMs.description: Per-provider request timeout (ms) before failing over to the next model.

tiermux.rateLimitCooldownMs.description: How long (ms) to skip a rate-limited provider before trying it again. Honors a provider's Retry-After header when present.

tiermux.usage.referencePriceInPer1M.description: Reference price per 1M input tokens used to estimate how much you would have paid on a paid tier (default mirrors OpenAI GPT-4o). 0 disables the dollar estimate. Used only for the 'est. $ saved' line in the chat footer — you are not actually billed this amount, since every provider TierMux uses is free.

tiermux.usage.referencePriceOutPer1M.description: Reference price per 1M output tokens used to estimate how much you would have paid on a paid tier (default mirrors OpenAI GPT-4o). 0 disables the dollar estimate. Used only for the 'est. $ saved' line in the chat footer.

tiermux.chat.typingSpeedMs.description: Delay (ms) between chunks of the simulated typing animation. 0 disables animation.

tiermux.completions.enabled.description: Enable Copilot-style inline (ghost-text) completions. Off by default — high request volume against free-tier rate limits.

tiermux.completions.model.description: Model id used for inline completions, or 'auto' to use a fast enabled model.

tiermux.completions.debounceMs.description: Debounce (ms) before requesting an inline completion.

tiermux.embeddings.enabled.description: Enable the optional local codebase embeddings index (adds a semantic 'codebaseSearch' tool for the agent). Build it from the ⚙ Models panel.

tiermux.embeddings.provider.description: Provider used to embed code chunks (uses that provider's API key). Google text-embedding-004 and Cohere embed have generous free tiers.

tiermux.embeddings.model.description: Embedding model id (blank = provider default, e.g. gemini-embedding-001 for Google).

tiermux.embeddings.autoContext.description: When the index is built, automatically inject the top matching code chunks for your message into each turn (so the agent gets relevant context without calling codebaseSearch).

tiermux.embeddings.batchSize.description: How many chunks to embed per request while indexing. Larger = fewer requests (helps with per-minute rate limits) but bigger payloads; lower it if the provider rejects large batches.

tiermux.embeddings.requestDelayMs.description: Pause between embedding requests while indexing. Raise this (e.g. 500–1000) if your embedding provider keeps rate-limiting the index build.

tiermux.useOpenCodeEngine.description: [EXPERIMENTAL] Use the bundled TierMux engine instead of the legacy built-in agent.

tiermux.engine.traceOcEvents.description: Log every raw TierMux engine event to the 'TierMux Engine' output channel. Use this to debug chat issues — turn on, send a chat, then check View → Output → TierMux Engine.

tiermux.cache.fileEnabled.description: Cache file reads by mtime so the agent doesn't re-read the same file multiple times in one run. Saves input tokens and rate-limit quota on free LLMs.

tiermux.cache.searchEnabled.description: Cache grep and workspace-search results for 30 seconds so duplicate searches within a run return instantly.

tiermux.cache.searchTtlMs.description: How long (ms) grep/search results are cached. After this window, or when a file is written, the cache is cleared.

tiermux.cache.researchEnabled.description: Run a lightweight pre-research step (grep, semantic search, diagnostics) before the first model call. Typically saves 2–3 agent iterations on code tasks.

tiermux.catalog.url.description: URL to a published CSV (e.g. Google Sheets → File → Share → Publish to web → CSV) that supplies the model catalog. Fetched in the background on startup; the last fetch is cached and used offline, falling back to the built-in catalog. Leave blank to use only the built-in catalog.

tiermux.mcpRegistryUrl.description: Optional URL to a custom curated MCP list (JSON array or {servers:[…]} in this extension's format). When set, it replaces the bundled 'Add a server' list shown when the search box is empty.

tiermux.mcpRegistrySearchUrl.description: Base URL of the MCP registry queried by the remote search box in the MCP panel (official MCP registry by default). Blank disables remote search.

command.description: stdio: executable to launch (e.g. npx, uvx, node).

cwd.description: stdio: working directory (defaults to the workspace root).

scripts.package:full: npm run fetch:binaries && npm run build && vsce package --no-dependencies
